cpSection: 4.0 Inspection Procedures and Standards; 5.0 Safety and Health

lessons.title: Inspection Procedures: Minimum Requirements
objectives: Identify the minimum required elements of a written PT inspection procedure
Review a procedure for completeness and workability before use

lessons.title: Documentation of Inspection and Test
objectives: Record inspection results completely, accurately, and traceably
Prepare inspection reports suitable for review and audit

lessons.title: Standards, Codes, and Acceptance Criteria
objectives: Locate applicable methods and processes within governing standards and codes
Apply acceptance criteria from specifications to evaluated indications

lessons.title: Capstone Workshop: Evaluate, Disposition, and Document
objectives: Perform a complete evaluation cycle: interpret, classify, apply acceptance criteria, and document
Produce a finished inspection record meeting procedure and standard requirements

lessons.title: Writing the PT Procedure: Required Content
objectives: Structure a written PT procedure from foreword through reporting of results
Specify the product description, test conditions, and detailed application instructions in a procedure
Define how test results are recorded, classified, and reported
cp105Topics: 4.4 Product (description or drawing, including area to be tested)
4.5 Test conditions, including preparation for testing
4.6 Detailed instructions for application of the test
4.7 Recording and classifying the results of the test

lessons.title: Safety and Health in PT
objectives: Identify toxicity and flammability hazards of penetrant materials
Apply precautions for ultraviolet radiation exposure
Use material safety data sheets (MSDS/SDS) to control workplace hazards
